finding_results.notes: Replaced Mort's aphoristic line ('Nothing that fits in your pocket does everything') with rougher practical torpedo-level talk that stays in the concrete hardware domain ('close enough isn't level... the whole thing drifts'). Cut the three-sentence narrator evaluation paragraph ('That was the kind of thing Mort said. It was not wisdom, and it was not a line built to land. It was just the shape of his mind moving through the world.') entirely. Nate's too-fast laugh at line 55 now carries the emotional hit without narrator instruction on how to score the line. Mort's revised speech fits his style bible profile: moderate contraction level, material-specific lexicon, practical observation without generalizable wisdom.

summary: Single finding addressed. Mort's shelf-aisle torpedo-level speech roughened from polished aphorism into practical hardware talk. Narrator evaluation paragraph removed so Nate's reaction carries the scene beat without editorial commentary. Net effect: two lines of dialogue revised, one narrator paragraph cut. No other changes to the chapter.
